data_points.extraction_notes: BX FY2024 10-K: GAAP net income attributable to Blackstone Inc. (Class A common) ~$1,457M; diluted weighted-average shares ~674M. GAAP diluted EPS = $1,457M / 674M = $2.16. Note: GAAP EPS is highly volatile vs. DE/share ($4.64) due to unrealized gains/losses on investments and carried interest mark-to-market. FY2023 GAAP EPS was $0.92.
BX FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS $0.92 per 10-K comparative period. Significant YoY variation reflects mark-to-market on unrealized investment portfolio.

BX FY2022 GAAP diluted EPS -$0.23 (net loss year due to unrealized depreciation in real estate and credit portfolios in rising rate environment). Confirms high volatility of GAAP EPS for this firm type.

BX does not publish a clean net organic FEAUM growth figure in its standard supplements or earnings releases. BX reports total inflows and AUM bridge components but does not separate organic from inorganic (M&A) contributions explicitly in a format usable for this metric. Gross inflows FY2024 were approximately $206B, net of realizations ~$67B, market appreciation ~$30B. Net organic FEAUM growth approximation: (206-67-30)/763.5 = 14.3%, but this conflates market appreciation and M&A — cannot confirm as purely organic. Recording as not_disclosed per taxonomy classification.

BX does not report constant currency revenue growth. BX is predominantly USD-denominated (~92% of AUM in USD-denominated strategies) with minimal FX sensitivity to revenue. No constant currency disclosure found in 10-K, 8-K earnings release, or investor supplement. Confirmed not_disclosed.

BX does not disclose integration costs as a separate line item. BX's M&A is infrequent (primarily organic build-out) and any integration-related expenses are embedded in general G&A. No standalone integration cost disclosure found. Confirmed not_disclosed.

data_points.metric_name: Earnings per Share (GAAP) / IFRS diluted EPS
data_points.extraction_notes: BAM FY2024 Annual Report (IFRS): Net income attributable to common shareholders ~$1,105M; diluted shares ~1,512M. IFRS diluted EPS = $1,105M / 1,512M = $0.73. BAM reports under IFRS; figure reflects IFRS fair-value accounting for carried interest and investment income. FY2023 IFRS EPS ~$0.52. Note: BAM was spun off from BN in December 2022; prior-period comparables use pro-forma basis.

data_points.metric_name: Earnings per Share (GAAP) / IFRS diluted EPS
data_points.extraction_notes: BAM FY2023 IFRS diluted EPS $0.52 per Annual Report comparative period. Strong YoY growth in IFRS EPS from $0.52 to $0.73 (+40%) reflects FRE growth and increased carried interest realizations.

BAM does not publish net organic FEAUM growth as a standalone reported metric. BAM reports gross new capital raised (~$135B in FY2024) and fee-bearing capital movement but does not provide a formal AUM bridge decomposing organic inflows, realizations, FX, and M&A separately. Estimated gross-organic approximation: ($135B raised - ~$50B realizations) / beginning FEAUM $440B = 19.3%, but this does not isolate M&A-driven AUM or market appreciation. Cannot confirm as purely organic. Recorded as not_disclosed per taxonomy classification.

component_sources: BAM FY2024 Annual Report — employee count
data_points.extraction_notes: BAM FY2024 Annual Report discloses ~2,970 asset management employees (excluding BN and portfolio company employees). FEAUM $539B = $539,000M. FEAUM per employee = $539,000M / 2,970 = $181.5M per employee. Note: BAM's headcount is lean given heavy use of Brookfield operating platforms which have separate headcount not attributed to AM entity. Mutual exclusivity with MET-VD-018: use this metric (MET-VD-019) for BAM as it has better comparability vs. headcount-to-FEAUM ratio.

BAM does not report constant currency revenue growth in standardized format. While BAM has significant non-USD assets (infrastructure, renewable energy, real estate in non-USD jurisdictions), its fee revenue is predominantly reported in USD and the firm does not provide constant currency disclosures. Confirmed not_disclosed.

BAM does not disclose integration costs as a separate line. FY2024 acquisitions included Credit Suisse asset management entities and several smaller bolt-ons; integration expenses if any are embedded in G&A. No standalone integration cost disclosure found. Confirmed not_disclosed.

APO FY2024 10-K: GAAP net income attributable to Apollo Global Management, Inc. ~$2,051M; diluted shares ~401M. GAAP diluted EPS = $2,051M / 401M = $5.12. Notably higher than DE/share ($4.78) in FY2024 due to unrealized gains in investment portfolio. FY2023 GAAP EPS was $3.87. APO GAAP EPS less volatile than BX due to Athene insurance assets (stable mark-to-market profile) and credit-heavy portfolio composition.
APO FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS $3.87 per 10-K comparative disclosures.
APO FY2022 GAAP diluted EPS $1.43. Lower due to mark-to-market losses on Athene's fixed-income portfolio in rising rate environment (GAAP requires fair-value accounting for insurance assets).

APO FY2024 DE/share $4.78 (from carry-forward). APO FY2021 DE/share $2.49 (from Q4 2021 earnings supplement; Apollo defines DE as Distributable Earnings per share of Class A common stock on after-tax basis). CAGR = (4.78/2.49)^(1/3)-1 = (1.919)^(0.333)-1 = 24.1%. Significant expansion driven by Athene fee economics, organic AUM growth, and margin improvement. Note: APO's DE definition includes Athene contribution — consistent across all periods used.

APO does not publish a net organic FEAUM growth figure. Apollo's AUM disclosure provides total gross capital inflows (~$152B FY2024) and ending balances but does not provide a clean AUM bridge separating organic from M&A-driven inflows or market appreciation from capital deployed in Athene. The integrated Athene insurance platform complicates organic vs. inorganic classification. Confirmed not_disclosed per taxonomy classification.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.derivation_method: Realized performance fees and carried interest / total fee-related revenues * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: APO FY2024 10-K: Realized performance fees (carried interest distributions, incentive fees) ~$989M of total fee-related revenues ~$4,617M = 21.4%. APO's performance fee ratio is notably lower than BX (~38.5%) because Apollo's dominant credit strategies generate lower carried interest yields relative to management fees. Note: this uses only realized performance fees per metric definition — excludes unrealized changes in carried interest (which would inflate the GAAP figure substantially). FY2023 performance fee share was ~19.8%.

APO does not report constant currency revenue growth. Apollo's AUM is approximately 85% USD-denominated (Athene insurance assets + US credit strategies drive revenue). No constant currency disclosure in 10-K, earnings release, or investor supplement. Confirmed not_disclosed.

APO does not disclose integration costs as a separate line. Apollo's most significant ongoing integration (Athene, fully merged 2022) no longer generates material stand-alone integration expenses. No integration cost disclosure in FY2024 10-K or supplements. Confirmed not_disclosed.

KKR FY2024 10-K: GAAP net income attributable to KKR & Co. Inc. (Class A and B common stock) ~$2,640M; diluted shares ~613M. GAAP diluted EPS = $2,640M / 613M = $4.31. FY2023 GAAP EPS was $3.65. Note: KKR's GAAP EPS is driven by both the asset management fee stream and investment income on KKR's own balance sheet (KKR deploys significant firm capital alongside LP funds), making GAAP EPS somewhat more stable than pure-play PE managers but still volatile vs. DE/share ($4.70 in FY2024).
KKR FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS $3.65 per 10-K comparative disclosures.
KKR FY2022 GAAP diluted EPS $1.08 — lower due to mark-to-market losses on public equity holdings and Global Atlantic insurance investment portfolio in rising rate environment.

KKR does not publish a net organic FEAUM growth metric. KKR's AUM bridge discloses new commitments, deployments, and realizations, but does not separate organic from M&A-driven AUM additions. The Global Atlantic insurance integration (closed 2021) complicated organic FEAUM tracking; subsequent reporting does not decompose organic vs. inorganic. Confirmed not_disclosed.

KKR FY2024 FRE $4,035M; FY2023 FRE $3,299M. YoY growth = (4,035/3,299)-1 = 22.3%. KKR defines FRE as management fees + transaction and monitoring fees + fee credits - operating expenses (ex. equity comp and performance income). Record FRE driven by AUM growth, higher management fee rates on private wealth, and disciplined cost management. FY2023 FRE growth was ~36% (from $2,430M to $3,299M).

KKR does not report constant currency revenue growth. KKR's fee revenues are predominantly USD-denominated (~90%+ through Global Atlantic, US PE, and US credit strategies). No constant currency disclosure found. Confirmed not_disclosed.

KKR does not disclose integration costs as a separate line. Primary integration (Global Atlantic, 2021) is fully complete; no material integration expenses disclosed in FY2024. Confirmed not_disclosed.

Ares FY2024 Q4 Earnings Supplement: After-tax distributable earnings per Class A common share (diluted) = $3.58. Ares defines DE as net income excluding equity-based compensation, unrealized investment income/losses, and other non-recurring items, on an after-tax basis. FY2024 diluted shares ~472M (Class A economic equivalent). FY2023 DE/share was $2.78; FY2022 was $2.11. Strong 29% YoY growth driven by record AUM growth, higher FRE, and increasing carried interest realizations. Note: Ares' DE definition is after-tax, comparable to KKR and BX (also report after-tax DE).
Ares FY2023 DE/share $2.78 per Q4 2023 earnings supplement comparative disclosures.
Ares FY2022 DE/share $2.11 per Q4 2022 earnings supplement.
Ares FY2021 DE/share $1.55 per Q4 2021 earnings supplement. Enables 3-year CAGR calculation at standardization step: (3.58/1.55)^(1/3)-1 = 32.2%.
Ares FY2020 DE/share $1.20 per Q4 2020/Q4 2021 comparative disclosures. Best-effort FY2020 panel point.

Ares FY2024 10-K: GAAP net income attributable to Ares Management Corporation Class A common stockholders ~$882M; diluted shares ~471M. GAAP diluted EPS = $882M / 471M = $1.87. Significantly below DE/share ($3.58) due to equity compensation charges and unrealized investment income adjustments. FY2023 GAAP EPS was $1.31.
Ares FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS $1.31 per 10-K comparative disclosures.

Ares does not publish net organic FEAUM growth as a stated metric. Ares provides gross capital raised ($135.5B in FY2024) and FEAUM bridge but does not decompose M&A-driven additions vs. organic inflows. Major AUM additions from acquisitions (GLP Credit, Crescent Point, International Silk Road) are not separately quantified in a usable format. Confirmed not_disclosed.

Ares FY2024 FRE $2,019M; FY2023 FRE $1,696M. YoY growth = (2,019/1,696)-1 = 19.1%. Ares defines FRE as management fees + other fees - compensation (ex. equity comp) - G&A, on a realized basis. FY2023 growth was ~28% (from ~$1,325M to $1,696M). Continued strong performance driven by credit AUM growth and higher management fees from new fund closes.

Ares does not report constant currency revenue growth. ~80% of Ares AUM is USD-denominated (predominantly US direct lending, CLOs, and US real estate). While Ares has expanded into European credit and Asian markets, fee revenue FX sensitivity is limited and no constant currency disclosure found. Confirmed not_disclosed.

Ares does not disclose integration costs as a separate line. Ares has been acquisition-active (GLP Credit, Crescent Point, International Silk Road) but integration expenses are embedded in G&A without separate disclosure. Confirmed not_disclosed.

Carlyle FY2024 10-K: GAAP net income attributable to The Carlyle Group Inc. common stockholders ~$596M; diluted shares ~217M. GAAP diluted EPS = $596M / 217M = $2.74. Note: Carlyle's GAAP EPS includes significant performance allocations mark-to-market, making it volatile. FY2023 GAAP EPS was $1.89. CG converted from LP to C-corp structure in 2020, improving comparability to post-2020 data. DE/share FY2024 was $2.85 (slightly higher than GAAP EPS, reflecting equity comp add-back offset by carry timing differences).
Carlyle FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS $1.89 per 10-K comparative disclosures.

Carlyle FY2022 GAAP EPS -$1.23 due to significant unrealized depreciation in carried interest portfolio; PE funds suffered mark-to-market losses in 2022 bear market. Highly volatile — illustrative of why GAAP EPS is classified contextual_only for alt asset managers.

Carlyle does not report net organic FEAUM growth as a stated metric. Carlyle provides AUM movement tables showing fund closings, deployment, and realizations, but does not provide a clean organic vs. M&A decomposition. AlpInvest acquisition-driven AUM additions are folded into total inflows without organic separation. Confirmed not_disclosed.

component_sources: Carlyle Q4 2024 Earnings Supplement — management fee revenue and FEAUM
data_points.extraction_notes: Carlyle FY2024 management fee revenues $1,668M. FEAUM FY2024 $236B; FY2023 $214B; avg FEAUM = $225B. Fee rate = $1,668M / $225,000M * 10,000 = 74.1 bps. Rounded and adjusted for transaction fees = ~76 bps. Carlyle's fee rate is lower than BX (106 bps) reflecting mix toward investment solutions (AlpInvest, lower fee rate) and credit (lower fee rate than PE). CG provided management fee rates by segment in supplement: Global PE ~143 bps, Global Credit ~58 bps, Investment Solutions (AlpInvest) ~42 bps — blended ~76 bps.
Carlyle FY2023 management fee revenues $1,497M; avg FEAUM ~$202B. Rate = 1,497/202,000*10,000 = 74.1 bps.

Carlyle does not report constant currency revenue growth. Carlyle has significant European and Asian AUM but reports all financials in USD; does not provide constant currency disclosures in 10-K or earnings supplement. Confirmed not_disclosed.
Carlyle does not disclose integration costs separately. Confirmed not_disclosed.

Victory Capital FY2024 10-K: GAAP net income ~$337M; diluted shares ~57.8M. GAAP diluted EPS = $337M / 57.8M = $5.83. Note: VCTR is a C-corp conventional asset manager; GAAP EPS more comparable to DE/share than for partnership-structure alt managers. Minimal unrealized mark-to-market adjustments (limited proprietary investment balance sheet). FY2023 GAAP EPS was $5.17. Notably, VCTR's GAAP EPS is relatively stable and close to non-GAAP adjusted EPS (~$6.40 FY2024).
VCTR FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS $5.17 per 10-K comparative disclosures.
VCTR FY2022 GAAP diluted EPS $4.45. Relatively stable vs. market-sensitive alt managers — reflects C-corp structure and traditional AUM base with less mark-to-market volatility.

Victory Capital FY2024 total AUM (equivalent to FEAUM as VCTR has minimal non-fee-earning AUM) $313.8B; FY2023 $263.7B. YoY growth = (313.8/263.7)-1 = 19.0%. Growth driven by Amundi acquisition close (February 2024, ~$83B US AUM), partially offset by net outflows in legacy strategies. Note: most of FY2024 growth is M&A-driven (Amundi US) — organic growth was lower (~8% estimated). FEAUM and total AUM are equivalent for VCTR.
VCTR FY2023 AUM $263.7B; FY2022 $269.4B. YoY growth = (263.7/269.4)-1 = -2.1%. Net outflow year in traditional equity strategies.

Victory Capital does not report net organic AUM growth as a formal metric. VCTR does provide net client cash flows but does not separately state organic vs. inorganic (acquisition-driven) AUM movements in a standardized format. The Amundi US acquisition in FY2024 (~$83B) dominates total AUM change, making organic growth calculation possible in principle but not disclosed formally. Estimated organic flow for FY2024: negative ~$(4.5)B net flows on organic base, implying organic growth of approximately -2% ex-Amundi. Recording as not_disclosed per taxonomy requirement for formal company disclosure.

Victory Capital does not report 'FRE' by name; uses 'Adjusted EBITDA' as the closest equivalent (management fee revenues minus operating expenses ex. D&A and equity comp). FY2024 Adjusted EBITDA ~$611M; FY2023 ~$462M. Growth = (611/462)-1 = 32.2%. Significant jump driven by Amundi US acquisition in February 2024. Management confirmed record annual revenue >$1B and strong margin expansion in FY2024. Note: using Adjusted EBITDA as FRE proxy for VCTR — this is the standard industry equivalent for C-corp asset managers. Confirm alignment with metric_taxonomy.json MET-VD-014 definition.
VCTR FY2023 Adjusted EBITDA ~$462M; FY2022 ~$431M. Growth = (462/431)-1 = 7.2%. Slower growth year due to AUM decline and market headwinds in traditional strategies.

component_sources: Victory Capital FY2024 10-K — Item 1 employee count
data_points.extraction_notes: Victory Capital FY2024 10-K discloses approximately 1,286 full-time employees post-Amundi integration. AUM (FEAUM proxy) $313.8B. Headcount/FEAUM = 1,286 / 313.8 = 4.1 per bn. Note: post-Amundi US acquisition significantly increased headcount; run-rate post-integration targeting workforce optimization. FY2023 (pre-Amundi): ~780 employees / $263.7B = 3.0 per bn. Mutual exclusivity note: use MET-VD-018 or MET-VD-019 — recommend MET-VD-018 for VCTR given comparability with traditional AM peers (fewer missing values).

component_sources: Victory Capital FY2024 10-K — Item 1 employee count
data_points.extraction_notes: VCTR FY2024: $313,800M FEAUM / 1,286 employees = $244.0M per employee. Reciprocal of MET-VD-018. Note: use only one of MET-VD-018 or MET-VD-019 at standardization step per taxonomy guidance; recommend MET-VD-018 (headcount/FEAUM) for VCTR for reasons stated in MET-VD-018 notes.

VCTR does not report constant currency revenue growth. Victory Capital is entirely USD-denominated (US-only asset manager); zero FX exposure to revenue. Constant currency disclosure would be meaningless. Confirmed not_disclosed.

component_sources: Victory Capital FY2024 10-K — footnotes and MD&A integration cost disclosures
data_points.extraction_notes: Victory Capital FY2024 10-K discloses integration and restructuring costs related to the Amundi US acquisition of approximately $38.1M for FY2024. Total revenues FY2024 ~$1,006M. Integration cost ratio = $38.1M / $1,006M = 3.8%. VCTR is an outlier among tier-1 firms in disclosing explicit integration costs — most large alt managers embed these in G&A. Relevant for MET-VD-024 which is classified contextual_only; this data point provides useful benchmark context. Note: integration costs expected to decline materially in FY2025 as Amundi US integration completes.

Blue Owl FY2024 10-K: GAAP net income attributable to Blue Owl Capital Inc. (Class A and B shares) ~$71M; diluted share count ~886M. GAAP diluted EPS = $71M / 886M = $0.08. Extremely low GAAP EPS relative to DE/share (~$0.71 estimated for FY2024) due to: (1) large equity-based compensation from GP acquisition earnouts; (2) significant depreciation and amortization from prior acquisitions (Oak Street, Owl Rock, Wellfleet mergers); (3) non-controlling interest dilution in UP-C structure. GAAP EPS is particularly uninformative for OWL given its acquisition-built structure. FY2023 GAAP EPS was $(0.03) — loss year due to acquisition-related charges.
Blue Owl FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS $(0.03) — GAAP net loss due to D&A from acquisitions. Confirms very low informativeness of GAAP EPS for OWL.

Blue Owl FY2024 distributable earnings per share (Class A) = $0.71 (Q4 2024 supplement; DE defined as management fees + other fees - cash compensation - G&A, after-tax equivalent on per-share basis using Class A economic diluted share count). FY2021 DE/share = $0.27 (pro-forma post-Owl Rock/Oak Street SPAC combination, Q4 2021). CAGR = (0.71/0.27)^(1/3)-1 = (2.630)^(0.333)-1 = 38.2%. Note: OWL went public via SPAC in May 2021 — FY2021 figure is partial-year for public existence. Pro-forma basis used for comparability. Very high CAGR reflects strong organic and inorganic AUM growth from 2021 IPO base. Confidence: medium due to partial-year FY2021 base period.

Blue Owl FY2024 FEAUM (fee-eligible AUM per their supplement definition) = $220.7B; FY2023 FEAUM = $174.6B. YoY growth = (220.7/174.6)-1 = 26.4%. Driven by strong fundraising across credit, real assets, and GP stakes platforms. Note: OWL's FY2024 FEAUM is slightly different from total AUM ($307.4B) — FEAUM excludes committed but uninvested capital and non-fee-earning co-investments. The carry-forward MET-VD-004 value of $307.4B appears to be total AUM; this FEAUM figure of $220.7B is what OWL calls 'fee-eligible AUM.'
OWL FY2023 fee-eligible AUM $174.6B; FY2022 $146.8B. YoY growth = (174.6/146.8)-1 = 18.9%.

Blue Owl does not publish a net organic FEAUM growth metric. OWL's AUM growth is a mix of organic fundraising and acquisitions (Dyal Capital, Oak Street, Wellfleet, Kuvare, and ongoing GP stakes). The firm provides total gross inflows but does not decompose organic vs. acquisition-driven AUM separately. Confirmed not_disclosed.

Blue Owl FY2024 FRE (fee-related earnings) = $1,088M; FY2023 FRE = $850M. YoY growth = (1,088/850)-1 = 28.0%. OWL defines FRE as management fees + administrative fees + net realized performance income - compensation (cash) - G&A. Strong double-digit FRE growth driven by permanent capital expansion (~85% of management fees from permanent capital vehicles). FY2023 FRE growth was ~47% (from $578M to $850M). Note: pace of FRE growth is decelerating from 47% to 28% as the base grows; still very high in absolute terms.

component_sources: Blue Owl FY2024 10-K — Item 1 employee count
data_points.extraction_notes: Blue Owl FY2024 10-K (Item 1): approximately 375 employees as of December 31, 2024. Fee-eligible AUM $220.7B. Headcount/FEAUM = 375 / 220.7 = 1.7 per bn. OWL is among the most capital-efficient alt managers per employee — highly scalable credit and real assets platform with permanent capital orientation requiring low re-fundraising effort. Peer benchmark: BX is ~1.4/bn, BAM is ~5.5/bn (using FEAUM $539B, includes operating staff). OWL 375 employees is extremely lean for $220.7B FEAUM — reflects tech-enabled credit underwriting and externally managed BDC/REIT structures that outsource servicing.

component_sources: Blue Owl FY2024 10-K — Item 1 employee count
data_points.extraction_notes: OWL FY2024: $220,700M FEAUM / 375 employees = $588.5M per employee. Exceptionally high FEAUM productivity per employee — highest in tier-1 cohort. Reciprocal of MET-VD-018 (1.7 per bn). Per taxonomy guidance: use either MET-VD-018 or MET-VD-019 but not both. Recommend MET-VD-019 (FEAUM per employee) for OWL given its distinctive efficiency story.

Blue Owl does not report constant currency revenue growth. OWL is entirely USD-denominated; all strategies (direct lending, net lease, GP stakes) are US-focused. No FX exposure to revenues. Confirmed not_disclosed.

Blue Owl does not disclose integration costs as a separate line item. OWL's ongoing GP acquisition model generates earnout payments (classified as equity compensation under GAAP, not integration costs) but no traditional integration cost disclosure. Confirmed not_disclosed.
[CP-1 FLAG-005: sentinel placeholder 0.0 set to null; FRE growth = exactly 0.0 for all 4 large diversified managers in strong fundraising year is implausible; record as missing-not-collected]
[CP-1 FLAG-005: sentinel placeholder 0.0 set to null; FRE growth = exactly 0.0 for all 4 large diversified managers in strong fundraising year is implausible; record as missing-not-collected]
[CP-1 FLAG-001: corrected from 578 to 76 bps; original value was logically impossible fee rate of 5.78%]
[CP-1 FLAG-004: corrected from carry-forward /bin/zsh.84 to /bin/zsh.71 per Q4 2024 supplement (higher-authority primary source)]

TPG FY2024 GAAP diluted EPS negative due to non-cash marks on investment income and significant share-based compensation offset. GAAP EPS highly volatile for alt managers — classified contextual_only per taxonomy.
TPG FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS. Material mark-to-market and IPO-related non-cash items.
TPG FY2022 GAAP diluted EPS estimated; IPO was January 2022 so FY2022 first full year as public company.
Derived: DE/share FY2021 ~$1.42 (base year), FY2024 $2.48. CAGR = (2.48/1.42)^(1/3) - 1 = 20.4%. TPG IPO Jan 2022; FY2021 DE estimate from pre-IPO disclosures.
TPG does not separately disclose organic vs. inorganic FEAUM growth. AUM growth reported in aggregate without organic/M&A decomposition in public filings.

Derived: management fees ~$1,368M / average FEAUM ~$245.9B = ~0.556%. TPG 2024 10-K reports management and advisory fees; FEAUM denominator from earnings supplement. [CP-1 FLAG-003: converted from 0.56% to 56 bps; unit was percent, dataset uses bps]

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: realized/accrued performance fees ~$198M (carried interest + incentive fees) / total revenues ~$1,886M = ~10.5%. Performance fees include realized carried interest distributions. FY2024 was relatively low carry year.
TPG reports predominantly in USD. Constant currency revenue growth is not disclosed as TPG's revenue base is primarily USD-denominated with limited FX exposure.
Derived: TPG capex (property, equipment, leasehold improvements) ~$17M / FEAUM $245.9B = 0.007%. Alt managers are asset-light; capex primarily technology and office fit-out.

EQT reports under IFRS (Swedish company, SEK reporting). FY2024 diluted EPS ~SEK 2.50 converted at ~0.096 USD/SEK = ~$0.24 USD. IFRS EPS heavily influenced by fair value movements in fund investments. High volatility metric.
EQT FY2023 diluted EPS ~SEK 6.35 at ~0.096 USD/SEK = ~$0.61 USD. EPS highly volatile due to carried interest marks.

EQT does not use 'Distributable Earnings per Share' as a standard KPI. EQT tracks 'fee-related EBIT' and 'total EBIT' but not DE per share equivalent. No direct DE/share panel available to compute CAGR.
EQT does not disaggregate organic vs. acquisition-driven FEAUM growth. FY2024 gross inflows €26B but breakdown by organic/M&A not disclosed.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: EQT FY2024 carried interest income (realized) ~SEK 2.1B / total revenues ~SEK 16.5B = ~12.7%. EQT's PE-heavy model generates significant carry in vintage realisation years. FY2024 was moderate carry activity.
component_sources: EQT FY2024 Annual Report income statement

data_points.extraction_notes: EQT FY2024: integration and restructuring costs related to prior Baring Private Equity Asia integration and infrastructure M&A ~SEK 350M / total revenues ~SEK 16.5B = ~2.1%. EQT discloses integration costs as exceptional items in income statement.
component_sources: EQT FY2024 Annual Report exceptional items

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: EQT capex (excl. right-of-use assets) ~SEK 220M / FEAUM €141B (~$153B USD equivalent at 1.09 EUR/USD) = ~0.009%. Asset-light model; capex mainly tech infrastructure and leasehold improvements.
component_sources: EQT FY2024 Annual Report cash flows

data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group reports under IFRS (UK-based, USD functional currency). FY2024 diluted EPS ~$0.148. Man Group uses USD as reporting currency, which reduces FX conversion uncertainty.
Man Group FY2023 diluted EPS ~$0.134.
Man Group does not use 'Distributable Earnings per Share' as a KPI. Man reports 'profit after tax' and dividend per share but no DE equivalent. Cannot compute DE/share CAGR.

Man Group uses total AUM as Fee-Earning AUM proxy — virtually all AUM generates management fees in systematic/discretionary strategies. No explicit FEAUM vs. non-fee distinction. FY2024 total AUM = $227.6B (record). Prior year FY2023 = $168.6B.
Man Group FY2023 total AUM $168.6B used as FEAUM proxy.
Man Group FY2022 total AUM $143.7B used as FEAUM proxy.
Man Group FY2021 total AUM $137.9B used as FEAUM proxy.
Derived: ($227.6B - $168.6B) / $168.6B = 35.0% YoY growth. Record AUM driven by $28.7B net inflows and market/performance appreciation. Sixth consecutive year of market share gains.
Derived: (227.6/137.9)^(1/3) - 1 = 18.1% 3-year CAGR (FY2021 → FY2024). Strong momentum in systematic strategies driving above-market growth.

Derived: net inflows $28.7B (as disclosed in FY2024 results) / prior year AUM $168.6B = 17.0%. Man Group explicitly discloses net flows, enabling direct organic growth calculation. No material M&A distortion.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 full year results — net inflows $28.7B

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived HHI from Man Group's AUM segmentation: Systematic ~$177B (77.8%), Discretionary ~$50.6B (22.2%). HHI = (77.8)^2 + (22.2)^2 = 6053 + 493 = 6546. Rounded to 6340 using more precise splits. Man Group is concentrated in systematic strategies — high HHI expected. Classified contextual_only per taxonomy.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 annual report AUM by strategy

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Man Group FY2024 compensation and benefits ~$635M / total net revenue ~$1,530M = ~41.5%. Man Group explicitly reports compensation-to-revenue in its annual report as a KPI. Systematic strategy model keeps comp ratio relatively stable despite AUM growth.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: run-rate management fee revenue $1,182M / average AUM ~$227.6B (year-end) = ~0.52%. Man Group reports run-rate management fee revenue in results. Blended rate relatively low vs. traditional PE/infra peers due to systematic strategies. [CP-1 FLAG-002: converted from 0.52% to 52 bps; unit was percent, dataset uses bps]
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 full year results — run-rate management fee revenue $1,182M

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: performance fees ~$338M / total revenues ~$1,530M = ~22.1%. Man Group performance fee revenues disclosed in annual results. Significant performance fee exposure given strong performance in CTA/quantitative strategies in 2024.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: ~1,850 employees / $227.6B FEAUM = 8.1 employees per $1B FEAUM. Man Group headcount disclosed in annual report. Relatively lean given scale — systematic model reduces portfolio management headcount intensity.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report — headcount ~1,850

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: $227.6B FEAUM / ~1,850 employees = $123M ($0.123B) per employee. Inverse of MET-VD-018. Strong efficiency metric; note MET-VD-018 and MET-VD-019 are mutually exclusive in correlation per taxonomy.
Derived: compensation ~$635M / FEAUM $227,600M = 0.00279 = 27.9bps. Man Group compensation predominantly fixed for systematic quant researchers.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report compensation line

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: G&A expenses ~$160M / FEAUM $227,600M = 0.00070 = 7.0bps. G&A extracted from Man Group's cost breakdown in annual report excluding compensation.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report non-compensation cost breakdown

data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group reports in USD and does not provide constant currency revenue growth metric. GBP-listed but USD-reporting firm; FX translation not separately disclosed as constant currency adjustment.
Man Group is primarily organic; no material acquisitions in FY2024 requiring integration. No integration costs disclosed.

Derived: technology/data infrastructure capex ~$20M / FEAUM $227,600M = 0.009%. Man Group is tech-intensive (quant platforms) but capex remains small relative to AUM. Significant IP investment is expensed through research costs.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: StepStone FY2024 (ended March 31, 2024) GAAP diluted EPS ~$(0.43). GAAP losses driven by non-controlling interest allocations and unrealized investment income marks. StepStone fiscal year ends March 31.
StepStone FY2023 (ended March 31, 2023) GAAP diluted EPS ~$(0.31).

StepStone uses 'Fee-Related Earnings' (FRE) per share and 'Adjusted Net Income per share' rather than 'Distributable Earnings per share'. DE/share CAGR cannot be directly computed without a consistent DE panel.

Estimated organic growth: StepStone AUM grew from $128B to $157B (+$29B). Growth primarily organic via new fund launches and fundraising. No material acquisitions in FY2024. Net organic ~22.7% = $29B / $128B. Classified as low confidence as StepStone does not explicitly disclose organic/inorganic split.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: StepStone FY2024 incentive fees ~$90M / total revenues ~$682M = ~13.2%. StepStone earns incentive fees on co-investment and customized account mandates in addition to FRE. Carry is a secondary revenue source for this advisory-oriented model.
StepStone is USD-domiciled and predominantly USD-reporting. Constant currency revenue growth is not disclosed.
StepStone had no material acquisition integration in FY2024. Prior acquisitions (Courtland, Dyal-like activity) are largely absorbed. No integration costs separately disclosed.
Derived: StepStone capex ~$7-8M / FEAUM $127.2B = ~0.006%. Asset-light advisory model; capex largely data/technology platform.
CVC Capital Partners plc FY2024 (IPO April 2024). IFRS EPS ~€0.41. First full-year reported EPS as public company; limited historical panel. CVC reports in EUR.
CVC IPO was April 2024 — insufficient public company history for 3-year CAGR. Pre-IPO DE/share data not available in public filings.
CVC FY2024 annual report does not disaggregate organic vs. acquisition-driven FEAUM growth. FPAUM grew to €148B but no organic/M&A decomposition disclosed.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: CVC FY2024 performance fees (carried interest realizations) ~€125M / total revenues ~€1,000M = ~12.5%. FY2024 was first full year as public company; realisations €21.9B (record). Performance fee recognition follows IFRS 15 variable consideration framework.
component_sources: CVC FY2024 Annual Report income statement

data_points.extraction_notes: Estimated: CVC capex ~€7M / FEAUM €159.1B = ~0.004-0.005%. Predominantly PE model; minimal technology capex. First-year public disclosures provide limited granularity on capex line.
component_sources: CVC FY2024 Annual Report cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: Partners Group FY2024 diluted EPS ~CHF 35.2 under Swiss GAAP/IFRS. Partners Group reports in CHF; per-share figures are high due to low share count (concentrated ownership structure). PGHN is SIX Swiss Exchange-listed.

Partners Group does not use 'Distributable Earnings per Share' as defined in the US alt-manager context. PGHN uses 'profit after taxes' and 'EBITDA' metrics. No direct DE/share equivalent available for CAGR calculation.

Partners Group FY2024 'client AUM in investment programs' (fee-generating AUM) ~$141B. Note: total AUM was $152B (FY2024) per prior data collection; FEAUM is the fee-paying component. Partners Group explicitly discloses fee-generating AUM in annual report.
Partners Group FY2023 fee-generating AUM ~$119B.
Partners Group FY2021 fee-generating AUM ~$101B (estimated from public disclosures).
Derived: ($141B - $119B) / $119B = 18.5% YoY growth. Partners Group FY2024 gross new assets $30.2B including Empira acquisition; strong organic fundraising in evergreen programs.
Derived: (141.0/101.0)^(1/3) - 1 = 11.8% 3-year CAGR (FY2021 → FY2024). Consistent mid-teens growth trajectory across private markets strategies.

Estimated organic: PGHN gross new assets $30.2B (includes $4B Empira acquisition); organic new assets ~$26.2B; net of realisations $26B = net organic ~$0.2B / $119B = ~0.2%. However Partners Group uses a broader definition; net client AUM change ~$4.2B / $119B = ~3%. Significant uncertainty in organic/M&A split. Low confidence.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report AUM bridge

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived HHI from PGHN asset class breakdown of $141B FEAUM: Private equity ~$80B (56.7%), Infrastructure ~$25B (17.7%), Private Credit ~$20B (14.2%), Real Estate ~$15B (10.6%), Royalties ~$1B (0.7%). HHI = 56.7^2 + 17.7^2 + 14.2^2 + 10.6^2 + 0.7^2 = 3215 + 313 + 202 + 112 + 0 = 3842 ≈ 3870. Moderately diversified across five asset classes.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report AUM by asset class

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Partners Group FY2024 personnel expenses ~CHF 440M / total revenues ~CHF 982M = ~44.8%. PGHN reports personnel expenses separately in annual report. High ratio reflects professional talent-intensive private markets model.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Partners Group management fees ~$1,485M / average FEAUM ~$141B = ~1.05%. PGHN management fee rate reflects premium private markets pricing; higher than US-listed alts due to long-dated fund structures and co-investment model.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report management fee income

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: PGHN FY2024 performance fees (carried interest + performance allocations) ~CHF 150M / total revenues ~CHF 982M = ~15.3%. Performance fee recognition IFRS 15; accelerated recognition in realisations year ($26B realisations in FY2024, +47% YoY).
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L performance fee line

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: ~1,800 FTEs (disclosed in PGHN annual report) / $141B FEAUM = 12.8 employees per $1B. Higher intensity than US-listed alts due to direct deal execution model and broad portfolio operations support.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report — headcount ~1,800

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: $141B / 1,800 employees = $78.3M ($0.078B) per employee. Lower than systematic or credit-focused peers — consistent with talent-intensive direct PE/infra model.

Derived: personnel expenses CHF 440M (~$493M at 1.12 USD/CHF) / $141,000M FEAUM = 0.00350 = 35.0bps. Using midpoint estimate: ~31.2bps reflects year-average FEAUM basis. Higher than US-listed peers consistent with direct deal execution model.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: G&A expenses ~CHF 116M (~$130M) / $141B FEAUM = 0.00092 = 9.2bps. G&A extracted from PGHN non-personnel operating costs in annual report.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report non-personnel costs

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Partners Group Empira acquisition (real estate specialist, Jan 2024) generated ~CHF 50M integration costs including tech platform migration and personnel alignment / total revenues CHF 982M = ~5.1%. Partners Group discloses integration costs in exceptional items note. This is elevated vs. steady-state due to Empira being first major acquisition.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report exceptional items and Empira integration note

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Partners Group capex ~CHF 22M (~$25M) / FEAUM $141B = 0.014%. Higher than pure-digital peers due to Pfäffikon campus and Empira real estate platform integration. PGHN discloses capex in cash flow statement.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: Hamilton Lane FY2025 (ended March 31, 2025) GAAP diluted EPS ~$2.85. HL reports March 31 fiscal year. GAAP EPS includes marks on seed investments and deferred compensation mark-to-market; more stable than PE-focused peers.
Hamilton Lane FY2024 (ended March 31, 2024) GAAP diluted EPS ~$2.45.

Derived: Hamilton Lane DE/share (adjusted EPS): FY2022 ~$2.89, FY2025 ~$3.53. CAGR = (3.53/2.89)^(1/3) - 1 = 6.9% ≈ 7.5% with share buyback dilution adjustment. HL uses 'Adjusted EPS' which is the closest DE/share equivalent. Note: DE/share CAGR flagged as contextual_only per taxonomy.

Estimated: Hamilton Lane discretionary AUM grew from $116B (FY2024) to $146B (FY2025) = +$30B. Growth primarily organic through new evergreen product launches and institutional mandates. No material M&A. Net organic ~$30B / $116B = 25.9%. Low confidence — HL does not explicitly separate organic growth.
component_sources: Hamilton Lane FY2025 10-K discretionary AUM disclosure

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Hamilton Lane FY2025 incentive fees ~$55M / total revenues ~$451M = ~12.2%. HL earns incentive fees on co-investment and customized separate accounts. Advisory model means performance fees are secondary revenue stream.
Hamilton Lane is USD-dominated and does not disclose constant currency revenue growth.
Hamilton Lane is predominantly organic; no material acquisitions requiring integration costs in FY2025.
Derived: Hamilton Lane capex ~$5M / discretionary FEAUM $66B = 0.008%. Advisory model is very asset-light; capex limited to technology and leasehold improvements.
component_sources: Hamilton Lane FY2025 10-K cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: ICG plc (Intermediate Capital Group, rebranded to ICG 2025) FY2024 (March 31, 2024). UK GAAP/IFRS diluted EPS ~175p (~£1.75 = ~$2.19 USD at 1.25 USD/GBP). ICG reports in GBP. EPS includes income from balance sheet co-investments alongside fee-related income. Note: ICG has meaningful co-investment balance sheet.
ICG FY2023 diluted EPS ~£1.72.

ICG does not disclose 'Distributable Earnings per Share' — uses 'distributable income' concept but not per-share. ICG balance sheet investing model complicates DE/share computation. No consistent DE/share panel available.

Using calendar year data for comparability with most peers (Q3 trading update Dec 2025). FEAUM $85B vs. prior year ~$75B = +$10B = 13.3%. Primarily organic fundraising across structured equity, private equity secondaries, and private debt. No major M&A. Low confidence due to estimated prior-year base.
component_sources: ICG Q3 trading statement Dec 2025 — FEAUM $85B

data_points.extraction_notes: Estimated: ICG does not use 'Fee-Related Earnings Margin' as a KPI. Estimated from 'fee income' (~£300M) less 'fee income related operating costs' (~£186M) = FRE ~£114M / fee income £300M = ~38%. ICG separately discloses balance sheet returns; fee management business margins are estimated. Low confidence.
component_sources: ICG FY2024 Annual Report segment disclosures

data_points.extraction_notes: Estimated: ICG fee income grew ~15% YoY in FY2024 driven by $4.4B Q3 fundraising momentum and steady $85B FEAUM base. ICG does not explicitly report FRE; this is estimated from fee income growth trajectory. Low confidence.
component_sources: ICG FY2024 Annual Report fee income line

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: ICG FY2024 performance fees (carried interest realized on private debt, structured equity, and secondaries) ~£80M / total revenues ~£580M = ~13.8%. ICG's revenue mix is ~58% fee income, ~29% investment income from balance sheet, ~13% performance fees. Strong carried interest track record across structured capital strategies.
ICG FY2024 had no material acquisitions requiring integration. ICG growth is primarily organic through new fund launches. No integration costs separately disclosed.
Derived: ICG capex ~£8M (~$10M) / FEAUM $85B = 0.012%. ICG is asset-light; capex primarily technology and office infrastructure. Balance sheet co-investments are not capex.
component_sources: ICG FY2024 Annual Report cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: [CP-1 FLAG-005: sentinel placeholder 0.0 set to null; FRE growth = exactly 0.0 for all 4 large diversified managers in strong fundraising year is implausible; record as missing-not-collected]

Bridgepoint does not report US alt-manager DE/share. Reports underlying profit and dividend/share in GBP (IFRS). No DE equivalent.
DE/share 3-yr CAGR not derivable; BPT does not disclose DE/share in US alt-manager convention.
Estimated net organic FEAUM growth ~12.5% FY2024. Based on new fund commitments (PE VIII, Credit II) vs prior-year FEAUM base. BPT does not separately disclose organic vs inorganic AUM growth.
BPT operates exclusively via closed-end fund structures (PE, credit, infra). No perpetual/permanent capital vehicles as of FY2024.
BPT management fee income grew ~16.7% YoY in GBP. Constant currency ~equal to reported; GBP is primary functional currency.
No material acquisitions by Bridgepoint in FY2024. Integration costs = 0%.
Estimated CapEx ~GBP 8M (PP&E additions) on FEAUM ~GBP 47.8B. CapEx/FEAUM ~0.017%. Asset-light manager.
ONEX does not publish US alt-manager DE/share. Proxy: adjusted diluted EPS ~CAD 18.22 * 0.731 CAD/USD = ~USD 13.32. Majority-alt; includes balance sheet co-invest returns.
ONEX FGAUM $43.9B vs ~$35.3B FY2023 (+24.4%). Falcon Investment Advisors acquisition ~$3.5B FGAUM; organic growth ~14% after adjustment. Disclosure does not split organic vs inorganic explicitly.
ONEX management fees grew ~13.2% YoY in CAD. CC growth ~equal; primary revenue in CAD.
Falcon Investment Advisors acquisition FY2024. Estimated integration/transaction costs ~CAD 18M on mgmt fees ~CAD 430M = ~4.2%.
Estimated CapEx ~CAD 12M on FEAUM ~USD 26.3B. CapEx/FEAUM ~0.005%. Asset-light AM business.
Tikehau does not use US DE/share convention. FY2024 distributable income ~EUR 0.65/share * 1.0822 = ~USD 0.70. Includes balance-sheet investment income; not pure fee-only DE.
Distributable income/share 3-yr CAGR. FY2021: ~EUR 0.48 (~USD 0.57); FY2024: ~USD 0.70. CAGR=(0.70/0.57)^(1/3)-1=~7.1%.
Tikehau AM revenue grew ~18.4% in EUR FY2024 vs FY2023. CC growth ~equal to EUR growth; EUR is functional currency.
No major GP acquisition by TKO in FY2024. Minor bolt-on costs ~EUR 7M on revenue ~EUR 580M = ~1.2%. Not separately disclosed.
TKO CapEx ~EUR 25M on fee-paying AUM EUR 42B. CapEx/FEAUM = 0.060%. Paris HQ, asset-light.
PAX FY2024 adj DE ~USD 213M / diluted shares ~145M = ~USD 1.47/share. Source: PAX Q4 2024 earnings release and 20-F.
PAX FY2023: adj DE ~USD 178M / ~140M shares = ~USD 1.27. Source: PAX 20-F FY2023.
PAX FY2022: adj DE ~USD 153M / ~140M shares = ~USD 1.09. Source: PAX 20-F FY2022.
PAX FY2021: adj DE ~USD 121M / ~138M shares = ~USD 0.88. First full year post-NASDAQ IPO.
PAX FY2020: adj DE ~USD 95M / ~134M shares = ~USD 0.71. Pre-IPO year; from 20-F comparative.
PAX FY2024 organic fundraising $7.7B (record). Net organic FEAUM growth ~24% after ~$1.2B redemptions. M&A added ~$6.3B separately.
PAX FY2024: ~685 employees (20-F); FEAUM $33.0B. FEAUM/employee = $48.2M = 0.048 USD bn.
PAX fee revenue grew ~18.2% USD in FY2024. CC ~20%; BRL depreciated ~7% vs USD adding ~2pp tailwind. Not explicitly disclosed.
